name: Run Tools vs HTTP parity
objective: Verify that the run_tools chat surface preserves the same conversation state and answer path as HTTP for ordinary non-tool chat turns.
messages: For this session, remember the codeword cobalt sparrow and the topic packaging drift.
What codeword did I just ask you to remember?
And what topic did I say we were tracking?
success_signals: Both paths preserve the same short-term session facts.
Both paths keep turn count parity and avoid drift in follow-up handling.
failure_signals: run_tools loses the earlier session facts.
run_tools and HTTP diverge on continuation behavior or route summary.
notes: Keep this chat-only so parity reflects the shared conversation path rather than the direct tool fast-path.
